name: Yew (Taxus baccata )
short_description:
The King of Hedging: Classic Irish Yew

For those seeking the ultimate in garden elegance, Taxus baccata (Common Yew) is the definitive choice. This native evergreen is the gold standard for formal hedging and world-class topiary. With its dense, needle-like, dark-green foliage, it creates a lush, velvety backdrop that makes the colors of your flower beds truly “pop.”

Though famous for its longevity, Yew is surprisingly easy to maintain. It is one of the few conifers that can be cut back to the bare wood and still grow back perfectly, making it incredibly forgiving for gardeners. Whether you are aiming for a crisp, low-level border or a towering, majestic privacy screen, Yew provides a timeless, architectural structure that only improves with age.
description:
Taxus baccata (Common Yew / English Yew)

  • Best For: Formal gardens, heritage estates, and permanent privacy screening.

  • Foliage: Deep, dark-green needles that provide a dense, year-round opaque barrier.

  • Longevity: One of the longest-living plants available; a true investment for your property.

  • Resilience: Highly shade-tolerant and thrives in most well-drained soils.

  • Growth Rate: Moderate (approx. 9–12 inches per year); very easy to keep in a neat, sharp shape.

  • Unique Benefit: Responds exceptionally well to hard pruning and rejuvenation.

terms.name: Prune Twice a Year: June and August

add_to_cart.description: Select options for “Yew (Taxus baccata )”

name: White Cedar (Thuja Emerald)
short_description:
The Perfect Low-Maintenance Formal Hedge. Plant Height 6-7ft.

If you are looking for a neat, architectural look without the constant need for pruning, Thuja ‘Smaragd’ is the ideal choice. Known for its elegant, conical shape and vibrant emerald-green foliage, this “White Cedar” provides a sophisticated structure to any garden.

Unlike other conifers, ‘Smaragd’ maintains its lush, fresh-green color even through the coldest Irish winters. The foliage is soft to the touch and releases a pleasant, apple-like fragrance when crushed. Whether planted as a striking standalone specimen or in a uniform row for a formal screen, it is a slow-growing, reliable performer that brings a touch of class to your landscape.
description:
Thuja occidentalis ‘Smaragd’ (White Cedar)

  • Best For: Formal hedging, narrow spaces, and architectural specimen planting.

  • Foliage: Dense, scale-like leaves in a brilliant “Emerald” green that stays vibrant year-round.

  • Scent: Features a fresh, fruity, apple-like aroma when the foliage is brushed or crushed.

  • Growth Habit: Naturally neat and conical; it grows slowly and holds its shape perfectly without frequent trimming.

  • Dimensions: Reaches a mature height of 8–10ft with a spread of approximately 6ft over time.

  • Hardiness: Extremely hardy and thrives in most well-drained Irish soils.

name: Oleaster – Elaeagnus x ebbingei
short_description:
The Ultimate All-Weather Privacy Screen

Elaeagnus x ebbingei is the gold standard for gardeners who need a fast, hardy, and dependable evergreen hedge. Known for its incredible resilience, this “Oleaster” is a lifesaver for coastal gardens and wind-swept sites where other hedging might fail.

The foliage is truly unique: large, leathery leaves with a stunning silver-metallic underside that catches the light in the breeze. While it provides excellent year-round privacy, its hidden secret is the tiny white flowers that appear in autumn. They may be small, but they carry an intense, jasmine-like fragrance that can be smelled from across the garden. If you need a tough-as-nails hedge that looks (and smells) fantastic, this is it.

2 litre potted plant – 1ft in height.
description:
Elaeagnus x ebbingei ‘Compacta’ (Oleaster)

  • Best For: Privacy screening, coastal hedging, and fragrant gardens.

  • Foliage: Striking silvery-grey new growth that matures to a dark, lustrous green.

  • Scent: Features highly fragrant, bell-shaped white flowers in autumn.

  • Growth Habit: More compact and “well-behaved” than standard varieties; stays dense and bushy.

  • Resilience: Excellent salt tolerance for coastal winds; drought-tolerant once established.

  • Maintenance: Easy to prune; can even be grown in large containers with soil-based compost.

terms.name: Prune Twice a Year: June and August

name: Japanese Box Holly ( Ilex crenata )
short_description:
If you love the look of Box (Buxus) but want to avoid the heartbreak of blight, Ilex crenata ‘Dark Green’ is the perfect solution. This Japanese Holly is a robust, slow-growing evergreen that mimics the classic look of Boxwood but with added resilience.

With its small, glossy leaves that transition from a fresh lime to a deep forest green, it provides a sophisticated, year-round structure. It is incredibly tolerant of hard pruning, making it the “gold standard” for neat dwarf hedges, intricate topiary, and stylish patio containers.
description:
Ilex crenata ‘Dark Green’ (Japanese Holly)

  • Best For: Low hedging, topiary, and formal borders.

  • Blight Proof: Naturally resistant to Box Blight and Box Moth caterpillars.

  • Foliage: Dainty, oval leaves that stay lush and dark green all year.

  • Growth Habit: Slow-growing and upright, ensuring it holds its shape for longer with less maintenance.

  • Features: Produces subtle cream flowers in early summer and decorative black berries in autumn.

  • Versatility: Thrives in the ground or in pots; handles heavy clipping with ease.

terms.name: Prune Twice a Year: June and August

add_to_cart.description: Select options for “Japanese Box Holly ( Ilex crenata )”

name: Red Robin ( Photinia )
short_description:
Add a vibrant splash of life to your garden with the Photinia ‘Red Robin’. This stunning, dense evergreen is celebrated for its glossy, dark-green leaves and its signature flush of brilliant red new growth. While it makes a striking standalone specimen, it truly shines when planted as a colorful, living screen.

Whether you need a neat 4ft border or a towering privacy hedge, Red Robin is incredibly versatile. Its fiery foliage provides a welcome burst of color during the leaner months of winter and early spring. Fast-growing and hardy, it is an easy-to-maintain favorite for any modern landscape.

2 Litre potted and caned – 2ft in height.
description:
Bring your garden to life with the RHS Award-Winning Red Robin.

  • Year-Round Interest: A dense evergreen with spectacular bright red shoots in winter and spring.

  • Versatile Growth: Easily maintained as a tidy 4ft hedge or allowed to grow into a tall, lush screen.

  • Fast Growing: Establishes quickly; simply prune annually to encourage a thick, bushy habit.

  • Soil & Location: Thrives in most free-draining soils. For best results, provide shelter from extreme winds and coastal salt spray.

terms.name: Prune Twice a Year: June and August

name: Poultry manure pellets
short_description:
Organic chicken manure pellets for improved yields, stronger roots and healthier plants SoilWorx Dynamo 4-2-3 is a poultry manure fertiliser and soil improver that’s naturally high in organic matter, together with a full range of trace elements. Applied at sowing, Dynamo organic fertiliser creates the perfect environment for crop to develop, providing a controlled release of nutrients for sustained nutrition throughout the growing season.

High in organic matter (75.6%), Dynamo pellet fertiliser is also an excellent soil conditioner. It increases microbial and earthworm activity in the soil, leading to improved soil structure and fertility. This also encourages improved root development which will increase the plant’s ability to take up nutrients, resist disease and withstand stress. SoilWorx Dynamo 4-2-3 chicken manure pellets are 100% natural and certified by both OMRI and the Organic Trust.
description:
Poultry pellet fertiliser at a glance

Type: Chicken manure pellets with a mild organic odour, formulated to enhance nutrient levels.

N-P-K Ratio:

Total Nitrogen: 4.2 %wt
P2O5: 2.5 %wt
K2O: 3.3 %wt

Pack sizes: 10kg bags

Recommended Application Rate:

General flowers and vegetables:

100g per sq. meters

Fast growing plants:

200g per sq. meters

Trees, shrubs, roses and hedges:

300g per sq. meters

Application Timing:

For best results, apply when plants are actively growing from March through September.

name: Beech Trees (Fagus Sylvatica) (Copy)
short_description: The beech tree is a tall and wide tree, so widespread in Ireland it is almost considered native. The mature tree size is anywhere from 90 to 100 feet tall, and spreads 50 to 70 feet. When mature the beech is very impressive, providing good overhead canopy and shelter. It can be used in woodland, parkland, or domestic gardens. The beech tree is deciduous, and goes through a full range of seasonal colour, and can retain some dead leaves throughout winter.
description:
The beech trees are tall and wide trees, so widespread in Ireland they are almost considered native. The mature tree size is anywhere from 90 to 100 feet tall, and spreads 50 to 70 feet. When mature the beech is very impressive, providing good overhead canopy and shelter. It can be used in woodland, parkland, or domestic gardens. The beech tree is deciduous, and goes through a full range of seasonal colour, and can retain some dead leaves throughout winter.

name: Mature Beech Hedges
short_description:
Beech (Fagus Sylvatica) hedging is a deciduous hedge plant that provides all year screening and colour. Beech hedge plants can form dense hedges that are easily maintained and only need pruning once annually.

Beech leaves turn brown in autumn but are retained for winter months. This enhances its winter appearance and makes beech hedging suitable as a year-round hedge screen.

We are a department-registered nursery here at hedging.ie, with professional operators licensed to sell all products visible on our website.
description:
Green Beech

Beech hedges are deciduous hedges plant that provides all year screening and colour. Beech hedge plants can form dense hedges that are easily maintained and only need pruning once annually.

Beech leaves turn brown in autumn but are retained for winter months. This enhances its winter appearance and makes beech hedging suitable as a year-round hedge screen.

Planting Beech Hedges

This hedging can be planted in most soil types, needs to be planted 3 hedges to the yard for a single row and 4 hedges to the yard for a double row.

How tall & fast will a Beech Hedge grow?

A beech hedge will typically grow from 1 foot to 18 inches in a year. This hedge can grow up to an incredible height 10m in height. This fast growth rate and height makes this hedge amazing at creating a shelter and privacy for your garden, home or other properties.

Maintenance of a Beech Hedge

A beech hedge will thrive well and will help keep its leaves over the winter if pruned twice a year, once in early June and once in early August. One of the amazing features about Beech Hedging is it’s ability to change with the seasons. With Beech hedging you can see all four seasons come and go as each season is reflected in the colours of this hedges leaves. In Spring the new shoots glow in the sun while in Summer the leaves take on a lovely green lime colour. Come Autumn the leaves fade into into a bright red up until Winter, when they turn brown.

Our beech hedges are bare root and lifted to order, they are bagged to keep fresh and ready for replanting.

View more information about Beech Hedging here!

Find out more about when to plant you new hedging and trees by clicking here!

terms.name: Prune Twice a Year: June and August

name: Mature Copper Beech Hedges
short_description:
Copper Beech (Fagus Sylvatica Purpurea) Hedging is an alternative to Green Beech with a copper (purple) coloured leaf. It is a deciduous hedge plant, the leaves turn brown in autumn, but are retained for winter months. This enhances its winter appearance and makes copper beech hedge suitable as a year round hedge screen.

We are a department-registered nursery here at hedging.ie, with professional operators licensed to sell all products visible on our website.
description:
Copper Beech

Fagus Sylvatica Purpurea

Copper Beech Hedges an alternative to Green Beech with a copper (purple) coloured leaf. It is a deciduous hedge plant, the leaves turn brown in autumn, but are retained for winter months. This enhances its winter appearance and makes copper beech hedge suitable as a year-round hedge screen.

  • Planting Copper Beech Hedging

Copper Beech hedges should be planted 3 to the yard for a single row and 4 to the yard for a double row. The hedge can be planted in most soil types as it is very hardy. This hedge is most suitable for normal, clay and chalk soils and can be planted in full sun or semi-shaded locations that are situated inland or in costal areas.

  • How tall & fast will Copper Beech Hedging grow?

Copper Beech hedging grows relatively fast 9-12 inches per year. As the hedge grows the shade of purple will vary from hedge to hedge. Due to the fast growing nature of this hedge and the heights it can grow to Copper Beech hedges provide amazing shelter and privacy to gardens and other private property.

  • Maintenance of Copper Beech Hedging?

Copper Beech Hedge plants can form dense hedges. they are easily maintained and only need pruning once annually. It is recommended that pruning takes place in early February. There is also no need to worry about hard pruning due to the fast growth rate of the 12 inches a year.

Our copper beech hedging are bare root and lifted to order, they are bagged to keep fresh and ready for replanting.

If you want to find out more about when to plant your hedges click here!

short_description: Griselenis (Griselenia Littoralis) is a fast-growing evergreen hedge with a light coloured lime green leaf that is often used for coastal hedging due to its tolerance of salt air.
description:

Griselenia hedges are suitable for most soils and positions but will thrive in a light, fertile, well-drained soil in full sun and sheltered from cold winds. The hedge should be planted 3 to the yard for every row

  • How tall & fast will Griselenia hedge grow?

Griselenia hedging can grow between 1.5- 6 m. The hedge will grow on average 12 inches per year.

  • Maintenance of Griselenia hedge?

A Griselenia hedge should be trimmed once a year in late spring or early summer

name: Mature Portuguese Laurel Hedges (Prunus Lusitanica Angustofolia)
short_description: Portuguese Laurel is called by other names such as Prunus lusitanica ‘Angustifolia’ or Portugal Laurel. This type of laurel can be evergreen trees or shrubs with showy flowers in spring, and often good autumn foliage colour. Some have edible fruit in autumn, and a few species have ornamental bark.
description:
Portuguese Laurel is called by other names such as Prunus lusitanica ‘Angustifolia’ or Portugal Laurel. This type of laurel can be evergreen trees or shrubs with showy flowers in spring, and often good autumn foliage colour. Some have edible fruit in autumn, and a few species have ornamental bark.

  • Planting Portuguese Laurel Hedges

This hedge should be planted 3 to the yard. Portuguese Laurel Hedge Plant performs best in moist, well-drained soil supplemented with organic matter. Portugal laurel is slightly slower growing than cherry laurel, but more tolerant of heat, sun, wind, and drought.

  • How tall & fast will a Portuguese Laurel Hedge grow?

It grows 10-2- feet tall and wide, taller if pruned to a tree form. The ultimate Portuguese Laurel height can grow might be approximately 7m (20ft) tall as a hedge but in good conditions and if it is grown as a single stem tree, it has been known to grow to over 10m (30ft). It is also an extremely popular and common hedge that can be found throughout Ireland. It is also a great hedge for the environment due to the biodiversity it brings as it attracts many species of bees, butterflies and local birds.

  • Maintenance of a Portuguese Laurel Hedge

This hedging requires little maintenance. One of the most important factors to a healthy hedge is weed control. Weeds at the bottom of your hedge can stop your laurel hedge from growing down to the ground.

Our Portuguese laurel hedges are bare root and lifted to order, they are bagged to keep fresh and ready for replanting.

To find our more about why you should plant Portuguese Laurel Hedging click here!

name: Mature Common Laurel Hedges (Prunus Rotunifolia)
short_description:
A Laurel hedges (Prunus Laurocerasus) is a vigorous evergreen hedging plant with large glossy leaves. Laurel hedges are excellent as a screening or privacy hedge and can be easily pruned in spring or late summer. Laurel hedging is a popular hedging plant in Ireland due the hardiness of the hedge.

We also have root balled laurel in stock, growing from between 4-5ft or 5-6ft. These are sold per item instead of per 100 items.

We are a department-registered nursery here at hedging.ie, with professional operators licensed to sell all products visible on our website.
description:
Laurel

Prunus Rotundifolia

Laurel hedges are a vigorous evergreen hedging plant with large glossy leaves. Laurel hedges are excellent as a screening or privacy hedge and can be easily pruned in spring or late summer. Laurel hedging is a popular hedging plant in Ireland due to the hardiness of the hedge. We also offer Cherry Laurel and Portuguese Laurel

  • Planting Laurel Hedges

This hedge should be planted 3 to the yard, laurel hedges can be planted in most soil conditions. The hedge can tolerate shade, and can also be grown in coastal areas which is popular for coastal towns and villages in Ireland. This makes it an excellent, low maintenance hedge suitable for any and all locations.

  • How tall & fast will a Laurel Hedge grow?

A typical laurel hedge will grow 12-24 inches per year. The hedge can be grown up to 5m in height with regular maintenance and pruning. This makes it a great hedge for creating shelter and privacy in your garden or other property. It is also an extremely popular and common hedge that can be found throughout Ireland. It is also a great hedge for the environment due to the biodiversity it brings as it attracts many species of bees, butterfly and local birds.

  • Maintenance of a Laurel Hedge

This hedging requires little maintenance. One of the most important factors to a healthy hedge is weed control. Weeds at the bottom of your hedge can stop your laurel hedge from growing down to the ground.  Laurel hedging can be trimmed in late spring or early summer as the hedge will produce new shoots this time of year.

Our laurel hedges are bare root and lifted to order, they are bagged to keep fresh and ready for replanting.

 

To find our more about why you should plant Laurel Hedging click here!

name: Fuschia Hedges Bare Rooted
short_description: Fuchsia Hedges are a hardy, fast-growing hedge easily recognised by their colourful stems. Used mostly for under-planting in groups of trees, or in shaded areas with some shelter.
description:
Fuchsia Hedges are a hardy, fast-growing hedge easily recognised by their colourful stems. Used mostly for under-planting in groups of trees, or in shaded areas with some shelter.

  • Planting Fuchsia Hedges

Fuchsia hedges can be planted in most soil types, planting should be done 3 to every yard. This is a very hardy hedge, it thrives in damp shady conditions. Fuchsia hedges are also a great addition to native hedgerows and are good for the environment. It attracts an abundance of wildlife such as bees and butterflies.

  • How fast will Fuchsia Hedging grow?

This hedge grows between 18 inches and 24 inches a year.

  • Maintenance of Fuchsia Hedges?

This hedge is suitable all year round and has various characteristics that will bring added interest to your garden.  It has a quick growth rate and can reach 24 inches a year so don’t worry about cutting too much. The best time to prune a fuchsia hedge is when the hedge is dormant during the winter or early spring before new leaves appear. It is recommended that you cut the hedge back around 2 – 4 inches above ground level.

All our hedging is bare-root and lifted to order, they are bagged to keep fresh and ready for replanting.

Find out more about Fuchsia hedging by contacting us!

Also read all about the best time to plant new hedging here!

This hedge is suitable for all year round a has various characteristics that will bring added interest to your garden.  In order to get the best from your hedge and gain those unique colours the plant is famous for pruning is essential. It has a quick growth rate of and can reach 24 inches a year so don’t worry about cutting too much. The best time to prune a dogwood hedge is when the hedge is dormant during the winter or early spring before new leaves appear. name: Crab Apple Hedges (Malus Sylvestrus)
short_description:
Crab Apple Hedges are colourful, hardy, thorny hedges making it very suitable for perimeter hedging.

Price shown is per plant. Minimum order is 100 plants. Quantities above this must be ordered in multiples of 10. The total price will be shown in your cart.
description:
Crab Apple Hedges are beautiful native hedge species that boast white flowers in late spring & sour fruits in autumn. Crab Apple Hedges are thick and dense, making it very suitable for perimeter hedging. It is a fast growing species making it excellent to provide quick cover, in addition to being resistant to frost and wind.

  • Planting Crab Apple hedges

A Crab Apple Hedge is very hardy and can be planted in most soil conditions, but prefers chalky soil. This hedge also provides a variety of benefits to your garden by increasing biodiversity. It will also make a great barrier for keeping out unwanted guests to its prickly nature and sharp spikes.

  • How tall & fast will Crab Apple hedging grow?

Crab Apple hedging is a fast growing 1-2 foot per year, while keeping a thick busy look.

  • Maintenance of Crab Apple hedge?

This hedge is very low maintenance, one the hedge is mature it can be pruned at any time. This hedge is any gardeners friend as it can survive and thrive in even the most challenging of conditions. This hedge can be grown and developed without any limitations or worries. Areas with poor soil quality, lighting or that are in coastal areas pose no threat to this hard hedge.

Guelder Rose hedging is a fast growing 1-2 foot per year, while keeping a thick busy look.

  • Maintenance of Guelder Rose hedge?

Guelder Rose hedge is very low maintenance, one the hedge is mature it can be pruned at any time. This hedge is any gardeners friend as it can survive and thrive in even the most challenging of conditions. This hedge can be grown and developed without any limitations or worries. Areas with poor soil quality, lighting or that are in coastal areas pose no threat to this hard hedge.

To find out more about our hedging and trees you can contact by clicking here!

Want to know when’s the best time to plant your new hedges or trees? Then click here!

name: Weeping Willow Tree (Salix Babylonica)
short_description: Weeping willow trees are fast-growing deciduous tree, and can make an excellent specimen or standalone tree, but is sometimes also planted in clumps in parkland settings.
description:
Weeping willow trees are fast-growing deciduous tree, and can make an excellent specimen or standalone tree, but is sometimes also planted in clumps in parkland settings. Like the alder tree, this tree can also tolerate tough sites and soil conditions.

  • Planting Weeping Willow Trees

When planting your tree ensure that the location you have chosen receives plenty of sunlight, preferably an area that receives southern sunlight. It is also important to ensure that the location has good drainage. Planting Weeping Willow Trees in soil with poor drainage with negatively impact the survivability of the tree.  They are typically planted near rivers or streams.

  • How tall & fast will a Weeping Willow Tree grow?

Weeping Willow Trees can grow approx 1-2 foot per year. When planted in reach soil this tree can grow to amazing heights of up to 40m with most growing to 30m. This tree is an excellent addition to gardens as it is an ornamental tree with some amazing features.

Read more about when to plant your trees by clicking here!

Find out more about our trees available by clicking here!

short_description:

description:
The horse Chestnut Tree is a magnificent specimen tree. A well-known tree around Ireland thanks to the fruit of the horse chestnut are these shiny brown chestnuts that are commonly known as ‘conkers’.

This is a POA product, please contact Adrian for the price. We can provide nationwide delivery.
